evidence_text: Given the strength of our business, we are raising our full-year outlook for revenue growth and free cash flow.
notes: Official IBM Q3 2025 release excerpt explicitly states the company is raising its full-year outlook.

evidence_text: We now expect CapEx to be in the range of $91 billion to $93 billion in 2025, up from our previous estimate of $85 billion.
notes: Official transcript-page excerpt explicitly states the updated CapEx expectation is up from the previous estimate; treated as a conservative raised guidance/outlook signal within the closed set.

evidence_text: Revenue is expected to be $65.0 billion, plus or minus 2%.
notes: Official NVIDIA Q3 FY2026 release excerpt contains explicit numerical outlook but no raised, maintained, lowered, or withdrawn guidance action.

evidence_text: We expect continued momentum from Windows 10 end of support, although growth rates will be impacted by elevated inventory levels at the end of Q1 that we expect to come down through the quarter.
notes: Official transcript-page excerpt contains forward-looking language but no explicit raised, maintained, lowered, or withdrawn guidance action versus prior guidance.

evidence_text: Based on our strong results in the first half of the year, which included the benefit of efficient project execution and favorable project timing, and while considering our project mix and expected project cadence for the second half of the year, we are raising our 2025 guidance. We now expect revenues to be in the range of $3.3 billion to $3.4 billion and EBITDA in the range of $240 million to $255 million.
notes: Official Everus Q2 2025 release excerpt explicitly states management is raising 2025 guidance and provides the updated revenue and EBITDA ranges.

evidence_text: With this positive momentum, we're raising our fiscal year 2025 guidance to project revenue between $1.69 billion and $1.73 billion, and adjusted earnings per share of $5.75 to $5.95.
notes: Official corrected transcript excerpt explicitly states management is raising fiscal year 2025 guidance and gives the updated revenue and EPS range.

evidence_text: Based on our strong first quarter results and continued favorable market outlook, balanced against economic uncertainties and typical project timing movement, we are maintaining our guidance for 2025.
notes: Official Everus Q1 2025 release excerpt explicitly states management is maintaining guidance for 2025.
